lines: Monica holds a slate across both palms while three chalk names dry on it.
"Lessons are between water trips. You have until the next jar arrives."

choices.label: Ask what she thinks of the census

lines: "Names and sums first, then pump marks with road signs. Poetry waits for a roof loud enough to hide it from practical adults."
"A child who can read a form is harder to erase with one."

choices.label: Ask what she thinks of the census

lines: "A census counts the people its boxes expect. My roll begins with who came through the door."
"The difference looks small until a child has no box."
